overall_assessment: Conditionally acceptable corrected baseline. The bounded package now shows strong structural coverage for fail-closed tenant auth resolution, reset-token hardening, and public-auth risk-matrix registration, but it still carries one material operational-fit gap around the deliberate burn-before-mutation-failure recovery contract.
recommended_path: Proceed only after resolving or explicitly waiving RR-AUTH-04-CRIT-001 in the acceptance ledger, then continue with the remaining required rerun lanes and closure guards already identified by the package.

findings.title: Reissue-only recovery is specified, but the package does not show explicit operational handling evidence for that failure mode
findings.rationale: The package makes token consumption intentionally fail-safe and states that any post-consume password-mutation failure requires a fresh reset token, but the bounded evidence stops at service/test semantics and does not show a corresponding operator runbook, user-facing recovery expectation, or explicit telemetry/assertion proving this branch is actionable in production. If this branch occurs in production, the security posture remains correct, but recovery may degrade into opaque support churn because users and operators only observe a failed reset plus an already-burned token.
findings.suggested_action: Keep the hardening, but gate closure on one bounded proof that the reissue-required path is operationally handled: either explicit support/runbook documentation tied to RR-AUTH-04, or testable telemetry/error-contract evidence demonstrating how the burned-token outcome is recognized and resolved.
